rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Relieving Petitioner From The Provisions Of Government Code Section 954.4; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Thursday, Apr-24-2025, LINE 1. PETITIONER BRADFORD SOLINSKY'S Petition For Order Relieving Petitioner From The Provisions Of Government Code Section 954.4. The unopposed petition for order relieving petitioner from the requirements of Government Code section 945.4 is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

Defendants Walgreen Co. and Walgreen Boots Alliance Inc.'s motion for protective order is granted. rulings.Case Title: DAVID MAR ET AL VS. KYLE TSE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Thursday, Apr-24-2025, LINE 8. DEFENDANTS KYLE TSE, and NANXI LIU'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Motion For Sanctions. Defendants Kyle Tse and Nanxi Liu seek an order compelling plaintiff David Mar to provide further responses to their Special Interrogatories, Set One, and for sanctions. Mar has not filed an opposition. The court grants the motion as set forth herein. Mar's objections to the following special interrogatories lack merit and he is ordered to provide further responses: Nos. 5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13. The court does not find any invasion of constitutional privacy interests with these interrogatories, the chief objection interposed by Mar. With respect to No. 6, the mere identification of documents exchanged with potential renters does not implicate privacy concerns, although the production of those documents might. Mar is ordered to amend his responses to these interrogatories within 10 court days of entry of this order. Tse and Liu also seek sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions are permitted even where a party does not oppose a discovery motion that is granted against it. (Rules of Court, 3.1348(a).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders Mar to pay $990 in sanctions to Tse and Liu within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KAMALDIP GHEI VS. SUTTER VISTING NURSE ASSOCIATION AND HOSPICE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Thursday, Apr-24-2025, LINE 4. DEFENDANTS SUTTER VISITING NURSE ASSOCIATION AND HOSPICE DBA SUTTER CARE AT HOME'S Motion To Compel Arbitration; **First Appearance Fee Paid**. Defendant Sutter Visiting Nurse Association and Hospice's motion to compel arbitration is granted on the terms set forth below. Plaintiff Kamaldip Ghei, M.D., was a hospice physician for Sutter whose contract was terminated. Ghei brings this action contending that his termination was in retaliation for refusing to extend a patient's stay at the hospice. Ghei worked for Sutter pursuant to a Hospice Physician and Coverage Services Agreement (Agreement). The parties agree that the Agreement prescribes arbitration for this dispute, but they dispute the effect of the delegation clause. Sutter seeks a stay of proceedings and an order compelling arbitration, leaving to the arbitrator all questions of unconscionability. Ghei asks the court to strike unconscionable provisions from the Agreement before compelling arbitration. ***THE FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
